message:
fix(raptorq,registry,streaming,store,sdk): overflow safety, DoS protection, and heartbeat correctness

Harden multiple crates against integer overflow and edge-case failures:

- raptorq/decode: replace unchecked addition with checked_add for
  projected memory calculation, preventing silent wrap-around that
  could bypass the memory limit on 32-bit or contrived inputs
- registry/lib: add u32 LE length prefixes to both signing_bytes and
  binary_hash in signature_message(), making the signature domain
  unambiguous and preventing length-extension attacks where a shorter
  signing_bytes + longer hash could collide with a different split
- streaming/sse: cap accumulated SSE data lines at 10 MB total,
  preventing a malicious or misbehaving SSE server from causing
  unbounded memory growth in long-running streaming sessions
- store/object_store: use saturating_add for quota check to prevent
  wrap-around bypassing the max_bytes limit
- store/repair: rewrite RateLimiter::refill to use u128 nanos to
  avoid precision loss, preserve fractional token phase across refill
  cycles, and prevent division-by-zero when max_tokens is zero
- sdk/runtime: fix is_heartbeat_timeout to base timeout on last ack
  elapsed time rather than last send time, which was incorrect when
  multiple heartbeats are sent — the old logic could false-positive
  timeout when a recent heartbeat overwrote sent timestamp

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
